identitySentence: Minor & Jester, PC is a Denton law firm founded in 1917 whose attorneys guide local families and businesses through real estate, family law, estate planning, probate, bankruptcy, and civil litigation matters.

concept.headline: Denton Counsel Since 1917, Ready When You Are
concept.subheadline: A divorce filing, a probate deadline, a landlord dispute, a bankruptcy decision: legal problems do not keep office hours, and reaching a lawyer should not have to either. Tell Minor & Jester, PC what is happening, and the firm will respond promptly with consultation times that work for you.
credibilityPoints: Serving Denton County clients since the firm's 1917 founding by Fred Minor
Senior partner Jill E. Jester has served on the Denton City Council since 2024
Partner Christopher B. Henry is admitted to all Texas state courts and three federal districts
Best of Denton winner (2012-2014) and Best of Denton County award recipient

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Minor & Jester, PC advises individuals, families, and businesses across real estate and landlord-tenant matters, divorce and child custody, wills and estate planning, probate and estate administration, guardianship, bankruptcy, business law, and civil and commercial litigation. Because the firm's attorneys work across these connected areas, your matter is evaluated from more than one angle.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Founded by Fred Minor in 1917, the firm has practiced in Denton for more than a century and today serves clients from 502 W Oak St in downtown Denton. Senior partner Jill E. Jester continues that civic tradition as a member of the Denton City Council since 2024.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: When you request a consultation, the firm will ask what happened, what documents exist, and what outcome you need so the right attorney can prepare. You will receive available appointment times, a short list of what to bring, and a candid assessment of whether the firm is the right fit.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Minor & Jester, PC,

A firm founded by Fred Minor in 1917 that still practices from 502 W Oak St has clearly earned its reputation the slow way: client by client. Your website already reflects that. It is modern, well maintained, and carries the firm's Best of Denton and Best of Denton County recognition. One thing it cannot do today is book a consultation. A potential client who finds you at 10 p.m. with a probate question or a custody concern still waits for a callback during business hours, and some will not wait.

That is the gap your Lead Capturer is built to close. We have prepared a custom intake page for Minor & Jester, PC that opens with your firm's own identity: "Minor & Jester, PC is a Denton law firm founded in 1917 whose attorneys guide local families and businesses through real estate, family law, estate planning, probate, bankruptcy, and civil litigation matters." Beneath it sits a contact form written around your practice areas, from real estate and landlord-tenant disputes to divorce, wills, probate, guardianship, bankruptcy, and business litigation, so the first thing a potential client reads sounds like your firm rather than a generic template.

Here is how it works. Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox with the full contents of the form. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to their message, so a landlord-tenant inquiry reads differently from an estate planning one. That response presents your available consultation times, and a $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so a booked time represents a committed prospect rather than a maybe. Nothing about your current site changes; the Lead Capturer works alongside it.

We would rather prove this than promise it, so we are offering a 15-day proof period. Test the complete experience yourselves. If it does not earn its place during those 15 days, there is no obligation to continue.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):
1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and likely matter type.
6. Confirm Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without staff action.
9. Confirm the customer receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

We will follow up briefly in about a week, and we are glad to talk sooner if you would like to see the page first.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
